_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **MONITORING** — Track legislative risk to IRA §60102 appropriation. The House Budget Reconciliation Bill, as introduced in May 2025, contains provisions that would repeal IRA Section 60102, which is the statutory basis for the entire Clean Ports Program. The workspace should assign a regulatory monitoring owner to track this bill's progress and model procurement contingency plans. No hard deadline yet, but the reconciliation process is active as of May 2026. *Source: IRA Section 60102 - Grants to Reduce Air Pollution at Ports, IRA Tracker, May 2025. https://iratracker.org/programs/ira-section-60102-grants-to-reduce-air-pollution-at-ports/*

- **MONITORING** — Assess funding-access status for any port partners that are Clean Ports Program grantees. Following the Trump administration's January 2025 IRA funding freeze (partially rescinded), grantees have experienced uneven access to ASAP.gov disbursements. The workspace should confirm with each port-side partner whether their grant funds are currently accessible before committing to project timelines that depend on zero-emission equipment being operationally deployed. *Source: U.S. ports fear that federal funding to clean up pollution could be canceled, Fast Company, March 2025. https://www.fastcompany.com/91301038/us-ports-federal-funding-pollution-clean-up-trump-administration*

- **MONITORING** — Review BABA (Build America, Buy America) equipment-ordering deadlines that govern grantee procurements. Under the EPA's targeted BABA waiver, the contracted order date for covered zero-emission mobile port equipment must be on or before **31 December 2027**, contracted delivery on or before **31 December 2028**, and actual delivery no later than **1 July 2029**. Workspace clients or port partners relying on waived ZE equipment should document order dates now. *Source: Clean Ports Program: Grantee Resources, US EPA, March 2026. https://www.epa.gov/ports-initiative/clean-ports-program-grantee-resources*

- **MONITORING** — Confirm semiannual progress-reporting cadence with any port-authority clients that hold ZE Technology Deployment grants. The first Clean Ports Program Progress Report covering activities through 30 June 2025 was due 29 August 2025; subsequent reports follow a semiannual cycle. The workspace should understand whether its port-side partners are meeting this obligation, as non-compliance could suspend disbursements that affect cargo-handling equipment availability. *Source: Office of Transportation and Air Quality Clean Ports Program Grants Q&A, US EPA, August 2025. https://www.epa.gov/system/files/documents/2025-08/2025-clean-ports-faq-2025-0822.pdf*
